title: Vibrant Chinese Dill Dumplings
description: Yeasty, bouncy-qq dumplings with fresh dill and a dash of Sichuan pepper, perfect for a hangover cure and served on a salt block for a smoky finish.
instructions: Mix flour, yeast, and salt in a bowl.
Add warm water and stir until a rough dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth, about 5 minutes.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 20 minutes until doubled.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of dill, scallions, and peppercorn powder in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over to form a half-moon shape and seal the edges with a pinch of sesame oil.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and drop dumplings in, cooking until they float and expand, about 4 minutes.
Serve the dumplings on a preheated salt block, drizzle with soy sauce and pour any pan juices over them.
